summary: Base64 decode pattern in @mastra/express bundle requires review
details: The package's bundled distribution files (dist/index.cjs and dist/index.js) contain a `Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString('utf-8')` decode pattern. This pattern by itself is benign and extremely common in JavaScript bundles — used for inline data URIs, JWT segment parsing, encoded SVG/PNG assets, configuration constants, and similar legitimate purposes. No corroborating evidence of credential theft, exfiltration, dropper behavior, lifecycle-script abuse, or hardcoded attacker infrastructure was observed. The match is on a single keyword pattern in a bundled main module with no traced harmful execution path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
